authoredShaderContract.waveCoordinate: Texture Coordinate Object → Mapping POINT rotation Y=1.5707963705062866 → Wave Bands X/SIN
authoredShaderContract.noiseCoordinate: Texture Coordinate Generated → identity Mapping POINT → normalized 3D Noise FBM

probeMethod: Blender 5.1.2 rendered the extracted Wave/Noise/Mix branch through Emission to OpenEXR after replacing only the two coordinate sources with explicit vector fixtures. The browser CPU oracle uses the same clean-room Blender-compatible noise core as the WebGL shader.

browserImplementation.objectCoordinates: Local mesh position, preserving Blender Object coordinates for the shaded object.
browserImplementation.generatedCoordinates: Local position normalized by the bounds of the complete evaluated object, not by each material primitive.
browserImplementation.bump: The shared Blender-derived finite-derivative Bump reconstruction evaluates the mixed height at the base and screen-space offset coordinates.
browserImplementation.replacedApproximation: Removed the prior hash noise, scale-20 object noise, 2π wave, multiplied phase offset, and omitted bump distance.

liveBrowserVerification.result: Both authored materials compiled and rendered in Shared WebGL preview with no shader error or warning.
liveBrowserVerification.repeatUpdateCheck: Three consecutive same-input evaluations completed with no console error after explicit geometry, material, texture, and program disposal was added.
liveBrowserVerification.expectedRuntimeWarning: The local Blender bake bridge was unavailable, so the page correctly used its baked Blender fallback.
liveBrowserVerification.materialIdentity: Source names 3D and 3D.004 remain intact; selection 5 reports 212 Blender and 220 GN-VM highlighted triangles, an eight-triangle tessellation delta.

rendererApproximation.status: material-semantic reconstruction, not pixel-identical Blender rendering
reasons: The clean-room WebGL noise helper and browser shader arithmetic do not preserve every Blender float32 intermediate.
Three.js MeshPhysicalMaterial lighting and normal integration differ from Blender Eevee/Cycles Principled BSDF.
The browser derives Bump through WebGL screen-space derivatives, so sampling and antialiasing remain renderer-dependent.
rendererApproximation.imageComparison: Pending a controlled matched-camera and matched-lighting Blender/WebGL capture; no pixel-parity claim is made.

ankermakeBed.sourceState: The extracted Blender image datablock has size [0, 0] and points to an unavailable external file.
ankermakeBed.browserState: The existing labeled procedural print-bed fallback is intentionally preserved until the original image is recovered.
